has been an annual competition of [ski mountaineering](/wiki/Ski_mountaineering "Ski mountaineering") at [Arêches-Beaufort](/w/index.php?title=Ar%C3%AAches-Beaufort&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arêches-Beaufort (page does not exist)") in the region of [Beaufort, Savoie](/wiki/Beaufort,_Savoie "Beaufort, Savoie") south-eastern France since 1986. The Pierra Menta is one of the three best known races of the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") (*les grandes trois de ski de montagne*) besides the [Patrouille des Glaciers](/wiki/Patrouille_des_Glaciers "Patrouille des Glaciers") and the [Trofeo Mezzalama](/wiki/Trofeo_Mezzalama "Trofeo Mezzalama").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Ski mountaineering competition

For the mountain, see [Pierra Menta (mountain)](/wiki/Pierra_Menta_(mountain) "Pierra Menta (mountain)").

The **Pierra Menta** has been an annual competition of [ski mountaineering](/wiki/Ski_mountaineering "Ski mountaineering") at [Arêches-Beaufort](/w/index.php?title=Ar%C3%AAches-Beaufort&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arêches-Beaufort (page does not exist)") in the region of [Beaufort, Savoie](/wiki/Beaufort,_Savoie "Beaufort, Savoie") south-eastern France since 1986. The Pierra Menta is one of the three best known races of the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") (*les grandes trois de ski de montagne*) besides the [Patrouille des Glaciers](/wiki/Patrouille_des_Glaciers "Patrouille des Glaciers") and the [Trofeo Mezzalama](/wiki/Trofeo_Mezzalama "Trofeo Mezzalama").

Pierra Menta is a stage of [La Grande Course](/wiki/La_Grande_Course "La Grande Course") that includes the most important ski mountaineering competitions of the season.

The athletes have to travel a cumulative altitude difference of around 10 000 meters, both ascending and skiing down. The race track is to be completed in teams of two and it varies year by year.[[1]](#cite_note-DAV-1) The distance for the younger classes is shorter.
